lines: Alaska is about to give away thirty one square miles of land.
For free. To a state agency called AIDEA.
It's birch forest near Houston. More than forty miles of mushing trails.
The plan: data centers. A rail hub. Power lines.
No company has signed on yet.
Houston's town council tried to ban it.
But their line falls two miles short.
AIDEA says it straight: no decisions have been made.
Comments close August nineteenth. Nothing here is final.
So the fence stands empty. The shadow keeps reaching.
The dogs still run this trail either way.
Nobody's said yes here. Nobody's said no where it counts.
